codeReview.summary: Backend invitation and role enforcement is mostly in place, but the frontend does not disable viewer editing interactions across all views as required.

issues.description: Spreadsheet cells remain editable (`editable: true`) with no viewer-role guard, so viewer users can still initiate employee edits. This violates the required behavior that viewer edit controls be disabled across all views.

Hierarchy rows are still draggable (`useSortable`) and inline editing remains active in this view, with no viewer-role guard. Viewer users can still initiate write interactions instead of seeing read-only controls.
Kanban drag-and-drop always calls `updateEmployee(...)` on drop without checking viewer role, allowing viewers to trigger status/department mutations.
Org chart drag-to-reparent still calls `moveEmployee(...)` with no viewer-role guard, so viewer users can trigger move actions even though the feature requires viewer edit controls to be disabled.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture documentation is stale after this feature and does not reflect invitation/member-role modeling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:40 and :53 still describe Organization as memberIds-only and omit invitation/memberRoles role model introduced by this feature.

summary: Review failed. The implementation does not fully satisfy the feature requirement that viewer edit controls are disabled across all views; several view-level mutation interactions remain enabled.
